_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to organise our data using tables, graphs and models so we can find patterns.

_source.w: We are learning to organise our data using tables, graphs and models so we can find patterns.
y: Organising data helps us see patterns and trends that would be hard to spot in a list of numbers.
Graphs and tables are a visual language — they help us communicate information clearly to others.
Scientists, mathematicians and many workers use data organisation every day to make decisions.
eg: Drawing a bar graph to show how far different toy cars rolled on different surfaces.
Making a table recording the temperature at 9 am, 12 pm and 3 pm each day for a week.
Creating a pie chart showing the types of minibeasts we found in the garden.
Using a column graph to display the results of a class seed-growing experiment.
Building a model of the water cycle using a plastic container, water and cling film.
Drawing a food chain to show how energy moves from plants to animals.
